rule: Tracked JSON, TOML, YAML, and YML files are machine-readable surfaces. Each must be classified as schema-backed, typed-validator-backed, generated/derived, source-checkout diagnostic, or an explicit freeform-prohibited gap, and must also declare a storage class explaining why the artifact belongs in checked-in state instead of being regenerated, reconstructed, kept local, or removed.

entries.notes: This inventory is validated by scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py.
entries.checked_in_justification: Canonical checked-in structured input used by package behavior, validation, packaging, or repo policy.

entries.notes: Checked-in candidate aids use one manifest per aid directory; scripts/check/check_agent_aids.py enforces nearby manifest coverage for aid files.

entries.notes: Lifecycle-created advisory proof route hints help proof selection avoid rediscovery while requiring live confirmation before command emission.
entries.checked_in_justification: Small advisory route cache retained to reduce repeated setup/adopt discovery; host policy remains in config and active planning.

entries.notes: System intent is compact repo-owned policy state.
entries.checked_in_justification: Compact durable decision state; reconstructing intent from chat or history would be lossy.

entries.notes: Subsystem intent is compact durable scoped decision pressure for package areas and host-repo components.
entries.checked_in_justification: Compact durable scoped intent; reconstructing subsystem direction from chat or archived plans would be lossy.

entries.schema_or_validator: planning surface checker recovery provenance validation
entries.notes: Transitional recovery provenance. Normal command-owned Planning mutations should be judged by schema validation, command output, and git diff; record-recovery is the explicit writer for emergency/manual repair.
entries.checked_in_justification: Checked-in recovery evidence used to distinguish exceptional manual repair from ordinary command-owned mutation while source-boundary gaps are being retired.

entries.notes: Generated Python command-package resource JSON files are adapter outputs derived from command_package_ir.json.
entries.checked_in_justification: Checked in so installed generated Python package resources load without embedding large JSON blocks in executable code.

entries.notes: Generated Python command-adapter metadata JSON files are adapter outputs derived from command_adapter_generation.json.
entries.checked_in_justification: Checked in so conformance and package proof can load generated adapter metadata as data instead of importing generated Python modules from source packages.

entries.notes: Generated Python operation-contract resources are adapter outputs derived from operation contracts consumed by generated CLI packages.
entries.checked_in_justification: Checked in so generated Python packages can load operation contracts at runtime without depending on source-tree contract paths.

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: installed planning module lifecycle and root maintainer-surface renderers
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Installed planning payload manifest used as the local source for generated weak-agent adapters.
generated_mirrors.freshness_check: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
generated_mirrors.ordinary_agent_route: agentic-workspace start --task "<task>" --format json or summary --format json
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote when installed planning lifecycle can reconstruct adapter metadata without a stable manifest path

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: weak-agent and source-checkout compatibility readers under tools/
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Compatibility mirror for agents that inspect tools/ before package commands are available.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: remove when tools/ weak-agent adapters are no longer a supported first-contact shape

generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Small generated compatibility adapter for agents that need a Markdown route before running commands.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: remove when AGENTS.md plus compact commands cover weak-agent startup without a tools quickstart

generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Small generated compatibility adapter for agents that need a Markdown route map after quickstart.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: merge into or remove after AGENT_QUICKSTART if secondary routing stops lowering startup cost

generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Package payload source needed to install the planning manifest into target repos.
generated_mirrors.ordinary_agent_route: package consumers should use installed AGENTS.md and agentic-workspace start, not this payload path
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote only if package install can generate the manifest from packaged contracts without a checked-in payload copy

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: package-local generated target bridges and installed package import proof
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated Python package metadata is kept as a peer target projection beside generated TypeScript outputs.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote to generated-on-demand only after packaging no longer needs checked-in generated Python metadata

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: package-local generated target JSON resource loaders
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated Python command-package resources are kept as files so executable adapters stay thin and do not embed large JSON blocks.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote to generated-on-demand only after packaging no longer needs checked-in generated Python resource files

generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated Python operation-contract resources are kept as files so runtime-consumed operations load from the generated package.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote only after generated Python packages no longer need packaged operation artifacts

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: generated Memory Python contract resource loaders
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated Memory contract mirrors are kept as files so runtime-consumed operations load package-owned policy IR from the generated package.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote only after generated Python packages no longer need packaged Memory contract artifacts

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: generated Memory Python payload resource loaders
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated Memory payload mirrors are kept as files so runtime-consumed operations load package payload data from the generated package.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote only after generated Python packages no longer need packaged Memory payload artifacts

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: generated Memory Python skill resource loaders
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated Memory skill mirrors are kept as files so runtime-consumed operations load package skill data from the generated package.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote only after generated Python packages no longer need packaged Memory skill artifacts

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: generated adapter conformance and package proof loaders
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated command adapter metadata is kept as JSON so proof can load it as data without package-local generated Python modules.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote only after generated adapter proof no longer needs checked-in adapter metadata artifacts

generated_mirrors.named_consumer: generated TypeScript package tests and package publishing preparation
generated_mirrors.checked_in_justification: Generated package metadata is kept as a reviewable adapter output while multi-language generation matures.
generated_mirrors.removal_or_demotion_path: demote to generated-on-demand once publishing no longer requires checked-in generated package metadata
